elements.sent-2411-001: In this note, we are interested in the probability that two independent squared Bessel processes do not cross for a long time.
elements.sent-2411-002: We show that this probability has a power decay which is given by the first zero of some hypergeometric function.
elements.sent-2411-003: We also compute along the way the distribution of the location where the crossing eventually occurs, as well as some Cram\'er's estimates for squared Bessel processes perturbed by a power drift.
elements.sent-2411-004: Let $X$ and $Y$ be two independent squared Bessel processes of respective dimensions $\alpha>0$ and $\beta\geq0$.
elements.sent-2411-005: We denote by $\Pb_{(x,y)}$ the law of the pair $(X,Y)$ when starting from $(x,y)$ and we assume that $0\leq x < y$.
elements.sent-2411-006: In this note, we are interested in the probability that the process $X$ remains below $Y$ for a long time : \begin{equation}\Pb_{(x,y)}\left(\forall s\leq t,\, X_s < Y_s\right)\qquad as  t\rightarrow +\infty.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-001: \begin{equation}\Pb_{(x,y)}\left(\forall s\leq t,\, X_s < Y_s\right)\qquad as  t\rightarrow +\infty.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-007: This question is often labelled in the literature as a capture or a pursuit problem.
elements.sent-2411-008: In most papers, the set-up is a Brownian one and the leading process is called a lamb or a prisoner, while its pursuers are wolves or policemen, see for instance \cite{Ken, LiSh, ReKr and the reference therein.}
elements.sent-2411-009: One may also see this question as a persistence problem for the difference of two squared Bessel processes.
elements.sent-2411-010: Indeed, a natural way to study such question is to introduce the stopping time \begin{equation}T= \inf\{t\geq0,\, X_t = Y_t\}\end{equation} and study its tail asymptotics since $\Pb_{(x,y)}\left(\forall s\leq t,\, X_s < Y_s\right) = \Pb_{(x,y)}\left(T> t\right)$.
elements.sent-2411-011: Unlike the sum of squared Bessel processes, the difference is no longer Markovian and studying such asymptotics is thus more delicate.
elements.sent-2411-012: We refer to the two surveys \cite{AuSi, BMS for an extensive background on persistence probabilities, as well as their links with many physic phenomenons.}
